instruction: How does the Big Bang theory explain the origin of the universe? response:
```markdown
## The Big Bang Theory Explained

The Big Bang theory posits that the universe began as an extremely hot, dense point approximately 13.8 billion years ago and has been expanding and cooling ever since. It explains the origin of the universe through the evolution of fundamental forces and particles, leading to the formation of subatomic particles, atoms, and eventually stars and galaxies.
